diagnostics.2: Be careful not to count the same object twice — try crossing them off as you go.
diagnostics.3: It looks like you forgot to count one of the groups. Read the problem again to find all the items.
diagnostics.correct_explanation: The answer is 142. When we count each item carefully — making sure not to skip any or count any twice — we get 142.

questions.original_stem: A box had 239 cubes. Builder Bee used 97 of them. How many cubes are left?

hint.level_0: Before counting, figure out: are you counting one type of thing, or adding up different groups?
hint.level_1: Try organizing what you need to count. Can you group items or use skip counting to make it faster?
hint.level_2: Count each group separately: 239, 97. Then add those groups together to get the total.
hint.level_3: Here's the solution: Counting everything gives us 142. Make sure each item is counted exactly once.

questions.visual_context: A 3D cube with visible faces and edges.
solution_steps: Identify what you need to count
Count systematically, left to right
The total count is your answer
